HomeWalksHistoryBathEat & DrinkStay

Town Story

History Highlights

A practical list of key historic stops in and around Bradford-on-Avon, with quick walking times.

Chronological landmarks

Short walk timings

Town + nearby highlights

Saxon Church of St Laurence

7 mins

Early 11th-century church; one of the finest surviving pre-Conquest buildings in England.

Photo: Spudgun67 (CC BY-SA 4.0)

St Mary’s Chapel (Tory Hill)

15 mins uphill

First recorded in 1533; the best view over Bradford-on-Avon.

Photo: Wilts24 (CC BY-SA 4.0)

Tithe Barn

15–18 mins

Built in the 1330s for Shaftesbury Abbey; among the finest medieval barns in England.

Photo: Rodw / Rod Ward (Public Domain)

Barton Farm Country Park

15–18 mins

Meadows, riverside paths, and the 14th-century packhorse bridge.

Photo: Betty Longbottom (CC BY-SA 2.0)

Town Bridge & the Lock-Up

3–4 mins

13th–14th-century bridge foundations; lock-up added in the 17th century.

Photo: Charles Miller (CC BY 2.0)

Iford Manor Gardens

Arts and Culture

Italianate design, tranquil terraces, and one of the area's most atmospheric garden settings.

Photo: Scott Wylie (CC BY-SA 4.0)
